explanation: Positive ADT and PADT on the left only (asymmetric) = Left AIC. Positive PART on the right is expected (lateral buttressing of right femoral head). SRT > 0 and Squat < 3/5 confirm not Patho.

repositioning.question: Which 3 repositioning techniques for Left AIC?
options: 90-90 Hip Lift in Passive FA IR with Balloon
Right Sidelying Respiratory Left Adductor Pull Back
correct: Right Sidelying Respiratory Left Adductor Pull Back
repositioning.explanation: Left AIC uses asymmetric repositioning. The B PEC/Patho set (Standing Wall Supported Reach, 90-90 in Passive FA IR with Balloon, Modified All Four Belly Lift) is bilateral.

postReposition.question: Post-repositioning: − L ADT, − R ADT, + L PADT, + R PART. What program?
options: Reevaluate technique or other PRI courses
postReposition.explanation: − L ADT / + L PADT ± R PART → Left AIC Pelvis Restoration. ADT cleared (bone repositioned) but PADT positive (pelvic floor can't ascend).

facilitation.question: First muscle group to facilitate in the L AIC Facilitation Hierarchy?
options: R anterior inlet (R rectus femoris + R sartorius)
facilitation.correct: R anterior inlet (R rectus femoris + R sartorius)
facilitation.explanation: Step 1: Turn ON R anterior inlet (IP ER) via R rectus femoris + R sartorius. R HALT 0/5.

explanation: Bilateral positive ADT/PADT (symmetric) with negative PART bilaterally = B PEC. SRT > 0 and Squat < 3/5 confirm standard B PEC, not Patho. Key: PART negative bilaterally.

repositioning.question: Which 3 repositioning techniques for Bilateral PEC?
options: 90-90 Hip Lift in Passive FA IR with Balloon
Right Sidelying Respiratory Left Adductor Pull Back
correct: 90-90 Hip Lift in Passive FA IR with Balloon
repositioning.explanation: B PEC and B Patho PEC use the same three bilateral repositioning techniques.

postReposition.question: Post-repositioning: − B ADT, + B PADT. What program?
options: Reevaluate technique or other PRI courses
postReposition.explanation: − B ADT / + B PADT → Pelvis Restoration. If both had cleared → Myokinematic Restoration & Postural Respiration.

facilitation.question: First priority in the B PEC hierarchy after repositioning?
options: Maximize inhibition of bilateral anterior inlet
facilitation.explanation: B PEC hierarchy step 1: maximize diaphragmatic respiratory function. Before inlet/outlet inhibition (step 2) and L AIC alternating program (step 3).

explanation: Patho = Paradoxical performance. Bilateral + ADT/PADT (like B PEC) but three distinguishers: (1) PART positive (B PEC: negative), (2) SRT = 0 / touches toes (B PEC: can't), (3) Squat > 3/5 (B PEC: < 3/5). Better ROM = worse pathology.

repositioning.question: Which 3 repositioning techniques for Bilateral Patho PEC?
options: 90-90 Hip Lift in Passive FA IR with Balloon
Right Sidelying Respiratory Left Adductor Pull Back
correct: 90-90 Hip Lift in Passive FA IR with Balloon
repositioning.explanation: Same three as B PEC. Treatment tracks diverge after repositioning, not at the repositioning step.

postReposition.question: Patient needs a balloon exercise but Squat is 4/5. What do you do?
options: Substitute a straw, kazoo, or party favor
postReposition.correct: Substitute a straw, kazoo, or party favor
postReposition.explanation: Functional Squat > 3/5 (Patho) → patient likely cannot handle a balloon. Use straw, kazoo, or party favor. Noted twice in the manual.

facilitation.question: First priority in the B Patho PEC hierarchy?
options: Maximize inhibition of bilateral anterior inlet
facilitation.explanation: Same as B PEC: step 1 is respiratory function. But for Patho, substitute balloon with straw/kazoo/party favor.

explanation: Asymmetric: + L ADT, + L PADT, + R PART. SRT uses hip-shift version (L AIC). Squat < 3/5, HALT 0/5 = early-stage L AIC.

repositioning.question: Which 3 repositioning techniques?
options: 90-90 Hip Lift in Passive FA IR with Balloon
Right Sidelying Respiratory Left Adductor Pull Back
correct: Right Sidelying Respiratory Left Adductor Pull Back

postReposition.question: Post-repositioning: − L ADT, − R ADT, + L PADT, − R PART. What program?
options: Reevaluate technique or other PRI courses
postReposition.explanation: − L ADT / + L PADT → Left AIC Pelvis Restoration. The R PART clearing doesn't change the pathway.

facilitation.question: R HALT is 0/5. Per the facilitation hierarchy, what first?
options: R anterior inlet via R rectus femoris + R sartorius
L anterior outlet via L pelvic floor muscles
L posterior inlet via L iliacus + L gluteus medius
R posterior outlet via R gluteus maximus
facilitation.correct: R anterior inlet via R rectus femoris + R sartorius
facilitation.explanation: HALT 0/5 = facilitation step 1: Turn ON R anterior inlet (IP ER) via R rectus femoris + R sartorius.

explanation: Bilateral + ADT/PADT. PART positive (unilateral — manual says 'bilateral or unilateral' for Patho). SRT = 0 (touches toes). Squat 3/5 is borderline but positive PART + touching toes clinches it. B PEC would have negative PART and can't touch toes.

repositioning.question: Which 3 repositioning techniques?
options: 90-90 Hip Lift in Passive FA IR with Balloon
Right Sidelying Respiratory Left Adductor Pull Back
correct: 90-90 Hip Lift in Passive FA IR with Balloon
repositioning.explanation: B Patho PEC → same three as B PEC.

postReposition.question: Post-repositioning: − B ADT, − B PADT. What program?
options: Reevaluate technique or other PRI courses
postReposition.explanation: − B ADT / − B PADT → Myokinematic Restoration & Postural Respiration. Both cleared = positional problem resolved, pelvic floor functioning.

facilitation.question: Both ADT and PADT cleared with repositioning alone. What does this mean?
options: Pelvic floor dysfunction was secondary to bone position — repositioning restored function
The patient has a Right AIC pattern
facilitation.correct: Pelvic floor dysfunction was secondary to bone position — repositioning restored function
facilitation.explanation: When both clear with repositioning, the problem was positional. The patient moves to Myokinematic Restoration — they don't need Pelvis Restoration.

explanation: Bilateral + ADT/PADT, negative PART bilaterally, SRT > 0, Squat < 3/5 = standard B PEC. If PART were positive, SRT were 0, or Squat > 3/5 → suspect Patho.

repositioning.question: 'Passive FA IR' in the 90-90 Hip Lift — what does this mean?
options: Pelvis internally rotates on a fixed femur
Femur is passively placed in internal rotation on a fixed pelvis
Patient actively internally rotates the femur
Acetabulum externally rotates on the femur
repositioning.correct: Femur is passively placed in internal rotation on a fixed pelvis
repositioning.explanation: FA = Femoral-Acetabular = femur is the moving bone. 'Passive' = clinician positions it. IR = internal rotation. Standard hip IR in an open-chain position.

postReposition.question: 'Promote inlet extension' means:
options: Move inlets from IP ER (flexion/open) into IP IR (extension/closed)
postReposition.correct: Move inlets from IP ER (flexion/open) into IP IR (extension/closed)
postReposition.explanation: In B PEC both inlets are in IP ER (flexion, abduction, ER = open). 'Inlet extension' = IP IR (extension, adduction, IR = closed). IO/TAs are the primary movers.

facilitation.question: B PEC hierarchy ends with 'Left AIC Alternating Program.' Why?
options: Behind every Bilateral PEC pattern is a Left AIC pattern
The left side is always weaker
B PEC only affects the left side
facilitation.correct: Behind every Bilateral PEC pattern is a Left AIC pattern
facilitation.explanation: The bilateral compensation started as a left-sided problem (structural asymmetry). After addressing the bilateral component, address the underlying L AIC.

explanation: Asymmetric: + L ADT/PADT, − R ADT/PADT, + R PART. SRT with L hip shift. Classic L AIC. Right side already partly neutral.

repositioning.question: R ADT already negative. After L AIC repositioning, L ADT clears. + L PADT, + R PART, R HALT 1/5. What facilitation step?
options: Step 1: R anterior inlet (R rectus femoris + R sartorius)
Step 2: L anterior outlet (L pelvic floor via adductors)
Step 3: L posterior inlet (L iliacus + L gluteus medius)
Step 4: R posterior outlet (R glute max)
repositioning.correct: Step 2: L anterior outlet (L pelvic floor via adductors)
repositioning.explanation: R HALT 1/5 = facilitation step 2: Turn ON L anterior outlet (IsP ER) via L pelvic floor muscles (via L adductors). The HALT score tells you where you are.

postReposition.question: All tests negative but patient is only 80% better. First thing to address?
options: Reciprocal L inlet IP IR / outlet IsP ER with inhalation and mid-zone with exhalation
postReposition.correct: Reciprocal L inlet IP IR / outlet IsP ER with inhalation and mid-zone with exhalation
postReposition.explanation: Neutrality achieved but symptoms persist → first: reciprocal inlet/outlet cycling with respiration. PRI Kegel is step 2 (requires HALT 4/5). Hormonal/psychosocial come later.

facilitation.question: PRI Kegel prerequisites?
options: Any HALT score with negative PADT
facilitation.explanation: PRI Kegel requires HALT 4/5 and neutral tests. Performed in L inlet IP IR / outlet IsP ER position.

explanation: Bilateral + ADT/PADT. Patho tells: (1) PART positive (unilateral on R), (2) SRT = 0 (touches toes), (3) Squat > 3/5. HALT < 3/5 is consistent with all patterns.

repositioning.question: ADT cleared bilaterally, PADT still + bilaterally, Squat 4/5. Manual says 90-90 Hip Lift with Balloon. Adjustment?
options: Substitute straw, kazoo, or party favor
repositioning.correct: Substitute straw, kazoo, or party favor
repositioning.explanation: Squat > 3/5 (Patho) → substitute balloon. Noted twice in manual.

postReposition.question: Pelvic floor in B Patho PEC vs. B PEC?
options: Patho: descended, bulged, high resting tone. PEC: descended, hypertonic, inflexible.
Patho: ascended and weak. PEC: descended and hypertonic.
postReposition.correct: Patho: descended, bulged, high resting tone. PEC: descended, hypertonic, inflexible.
postReposition.explanation: Both descended. B PEC: hypertonic/inflexible (stuck, still has tone). Patho: bulged outward under pressure, high resting tone (muscles failing to resist IAP).

facilitation.question: B Patho PEC hierarchy step 3?
facilitation.explanation: Both B PEC and B Patho PEC hierarchies end with L AIC Alternating Program — because behind every bilateral PEC is a Left AIC.
